# Channel.io App SDK

> TypeScript SDK for building Channel.io apps with the extension system

## Quick Start

```bash
npm install @channel.io/app-sdk
npx @channel.io/app-sdk create my-app --template=basic
```

## What is this SDK?

This SDK helps developers quickly build Channel.io apps by providing:

- Version-specific extension implementation (v1, v2, etc.)
- NestJS framework integration with auto-registration
- WAM (Web App Module) React utilities
- Token management with automatic caching and refresh
- Signature verification for secure requests
- Native function client for calling Channel App platform APIs
- CLI tools for project scaffolding

## Core Concepts

### Extensions

Extensions are predefined function collections with version-specific APIs. Available extensions (all V1):

- `createOAuthExtensionV1` - OAuth 2.0 authentication
- `createApiKeyExtensionV1` - API key authentication
- `createCalendarExtensionV1` - Calendar booking system
- `command` - Chat slash commands via `extension.command.metadata.getCommands`
- `widget` - Widgets via `extension.widget.metadata.getWidgets`
- `customtab` - Custom desk tabs via `extension.customtab.metadata.getCustomTabs`
- `hook` - Lifecycle hooks via `extension.hook.metadata.getHooks`
- `createAlfTaskExtensionV1` - ALF AI agent automation tasks
- `createNotebookExtensionV1` - App-managed notebook definitions

### System Version

Each extension has a `systemVersion` (e.g., "v1", "v2") that determines the API contract.

- Extensions with the same name but different versions can coexist
- The endpoint includes version: `PUT /functions/v1`, `PUT /functions/v2`
- Future v2 extensions will be breaking changes with new factory functions

### Function Naming

Functions follow: `{groupName}.{functionName}`
Full path in app store: `extension.{extensionName}.{groupName}.{functionName}`

### Metadata-Driven Registration

`command`, `widget`, `customtab`, and `hook` are registered by calling
`registerExtension(extensionName, "v1")`. During registration, AppStore calls
their metadata functions and persists the returned definitions.

- Command metadata: `extension.command.metadata.getCommands`
- Widget metadata: `extension.widget.metadata.getWidgets`
- Custom tab metadata: `extension.customtab.metadata.getCustomTabs`
- Hook metadata: `extension.hook.metadata.getHooks`

For these extension types, UI/action handlers are plain app functions referenced
by metadata fields such as `actionFunctionName`.

## Server SDK (NestJS)

### Define an Extension

```typescript
import { createCalendarExtensionV1 } from "@channel.io/app-sdk-server";

export const calendarExtension = createCalendarExtensionV1({
  listEventTypes: async (ctx, params) => ({
    eventTypes: [{ id: "1", name: "30 Minute Meeting", duration: 30 }],
  }),
  getAvailability: async (ctx, params) => ({ slots: [] }),
  createBooking: async (ctx, params) => {
    // Create booking logic
    return { booking: { id: "123", status: "confirmed" } };
  },
});
```

For metadata-driven extensions such as `command`, `widget`, `customtab`, and `hook`,
define the extension with `createExtension()` / `defineFunction()` and expose a
metadata function plus any referenced action functions:

```typescript
import {
  createExtension,
  defineFunction,
  GetWidgetsOutputSchema,
  WidgetActionResultSchema,
} from "@channel.io/app-sdk";
import { z } from "zod";

export const widgetExtension = createExtension({
  name: "widget",
  systemVersion: "v1",
  groups: {
    metadata: {
      getWidgets: defineFunction({
        input: z.object({}),
        output: GetWidgetsOutputSchema,
        handler: async () => ({
          widgets: [
            {
              name: "quick_actions",
              scope: "desk",
              widgetType: "wam",
              actionFunctionName: "extension.widget.widgets.open",
              defaultName: "Quick Actions",
            },
          ],
        }),
      }),
    },
    widgets: {
      open: defineFunction({
        input: z.object({
          chat: z.object({ type: z.string(), id: z.string() }),
          language: z.string(),
        }),
        output: WidgetActionResultSchema,
        handler: async (_ctx, params) => ({
          type: "wam",
          attributes: {
            appId: process.env.APP_ID,
            name: "quick-actions",
            wamArgs: { chatId: params.chat.id },
          },
        }),
      }),
    },
  },
});
```

Hook metadata uses flat items shaped like:

```typescript
{
  type: "app.installed" | "app.uninstalled" | "command.toggle" | "config.saved" | "config.deleted" | "widget.installed" | "widget.uninstalled";
  targetId?: string; // required for widget.* hooks
  actionFunctionName: string;
  systemVersion?: string;
}
```

### Register in NestJS (with Auto-Registration)

```typescript
import { Module } from "@nestjs/common";
import { ChannelAppModule } from "@channel.io/app-sdk-server";
import { calendarExtension } from "./extensions/calendar";

@Module({
  imports: [
    ChannelAppModule.forRoot({
      appId: process.env.APP_ID,
      appSecret: process.env.APP_SECRET,
      extensions: [calendarExtension],
      // Auto-register extensions with Channel App platform on startup
      autoRegister: true,
      // Optional: Custom AppStore URL
      appStoreUrl: "https://app-store.channel.io",
      // Optional: Callback after registration
      onAutoRegister: (results) => {
        results.forEach((r) => {
          console.log(`${r.extensionName}: ${r.success ? "OK" : r.error}`);
        });
      },
    }),
  ],
})
export class AppModule {}
```

### Versioned Endpoints

The SDK automatically serves extensions at versioned endpoints:

- `PUT /functions/v1` - for v1 extensions
- `PUT /functions/v2` - for v2 extensions (future)

Channel App platform calls `{functionURL}/{systemVersion}` (e.g., `https://your-app.com/functions/v1`).

## Signature Verification

Channel App platform signs requests with HMAC-SHA256. Use the `SignatureGuard` to verify:

### Setup Raw Body Parsing

```typescript
// main.ts
import express from "express";
import { NestFactory } from "@nestjs/core";
import { jsonWithRawBody } from "@channel.io/app-sdk-server";

const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule, {
  bodyParser: false,
});

// Capture raw body for signature verification
app.use(express.json(jsonWithRawBody()));
```

### Use SignatureGuard

```typescript
import { Module } from "@nestjs/common";
import { APP_GUARD } from "@nestjs/core";
import { ChannelAppModule, SignatureGuard } from "@channel.io/app-sdk-server";

@Module({
  imports: [
    ChannelAppModule.forRoot({
      appId: process.env.APP_ID,
      appSecret: process.env.APP_SECRET,
      signingKey: process.env.SIGNING_KEY, // 64-char hex string
      extensions: [calendarExtension],
    }),
  ],
  providers: [
    // Apply signature verification globally
    { provide: APP_GUARD, useClass: SignatureGuard },
  ],
})
export class AppModule {}
```

### Signature Algorithm

- Algorithm: HMAC-SHA256, Base64 encoded
- Signed data: Raw request body only
- Key format: 64-character hex string (decoded to bytes)

## Token Management

The SDK provides `TokenManager` for handling Channel.io API tokens with automatic caching and refresh.

### Basic Usage

```typescript
import { TokenManager } from "@channel.io/app-sdk-server";

const tokenManager = new TokenManager({
  appId: process.env.APP_ID,
  appSecret: process.env.APP_SECRET,
});

// Get a manager token (with automatic caching and refresh)
const token = await tokenManager.getManagerToken({
  channelId: "channel-123",
  managerId: "manager-456",
});

// Use the token for API calls
await fetch("https://api.channel.io/open/v5/...", {
  headers: {
    Authorization: `Bearer ${token.accessToken}`,
  },
});
```

### Token Types

```typescript
// Manager token - for desk/agent operations
const managerToken = await tokenManager.getManagerToken({
  channelId: "channel-123",
  managerId: "manager-456",
});

// User token - for user-facing operations
const userToken = await tokenManager.getUserToken({
  channelId: "channel-123",
  userId: "user-789",
});

// Channel token - for channel-level operations
const channelToken = await tokenManager.getChannelToken({
  channelId: "channel-123",
});
```

### Features

- **Automatic Caching**: Tokens are cached in memory (or custom storage)
- **Automatic Refresh**: Tokens are refreshed 5 minutes before expiry
- **Custom Storage**: Implement `TokenCacheStorage` interface for Redis/database
- **Error Handling**: `TokenManagerError` with status code and response body

## Native Function Client

Call Channel App platform native functions from your server:

```typescript
import { NativeFunctionClient } from "@channel.io/app-sdk-server";

const client = new NativeFunctionClient({
  appId: process.env.APP_ID,
  appSecret: process.env.APP_SECRET,
});

// Issue a token (no auth required)
const token = await client.issueToken({ channelId: "channel-123" });

// Write system logs (requires token)
await client.writeSystemLog(
  {
    channelId: "channel-123",
    logs: [{ action: "booking_created", metadata: { bookingId: "123" } }],
  },
  token.accessToken
);

// Get API key credentials
const creds = await client.getAPIKeyCredentials("channel-123", token.accessToken);

// Call any app function
const result = await client.callAppFunction(
  "target-app-id",
  "extension.calendar.booking.createBooking",
  { eventTypeId: "1", startTime: "2024-01-01T10:00:00Z" },
  { channel: { id: "channel-123" }, caller: { type: "manager", id: "mgr-1" } },
  token.accessToken,
  "v1"
);
```

## AppStore Client

Register/unregister extensions programmatically:

```typescript
import { AppStoreClient } from "@channel.io/app-sdk-server";

const client = new AppStoreClient({
  appId: process.env.APP_ID,
  appSecret: process.env.APP_SECRET,
});

// Register a single extension
const result = await client.registerExtension({
  extensionName: "calendar",
  systemVersion: "v1",
});

// Register multiple extensions
const results = await client.registerExtensions([
  { extensionName: "calendar", systemVersion: "v1" },
  { extensionName: "oauth", systemVersion: "v1" },
]);

// Unregister an extension
await client.unregisterExtension({
  extensionName: "calendar",
  systemVersion: "v1",
});
```

## WAM SDK (React)

### Access WAM Data

```typescript
import { useWamData, useWamSize } from "@channel.io/app-sdk-wam";

function Widget() {
  const appId = useWamData("appId");
  const { setSize } = useWamSize();

  useEffect(() => {
    setSize({ height: 400 });
  }, []);
}
```

### Call Server Functions

```typescript
import { useCallFunction } from "@channel.io/app-sdk-wam";

function Widget() {
  const { call, loading } = useCallFunction({
    appId: useWamData("appId"),
    name: "calendar.booking.createBooking",
  });

  const handleSubmit = () => call({ eventTypeId: "123", startTime: "..." });
}
```

## CLI Commands

```bash
# Create new app
npx @channel.io/app-sdk create <name> --template=<template>

# Add extension
npx @channel.io/app-sdk add extension <name>

# Development server
npx @channel.io/app-sdk dev

# Production build
npx @channel.io/app-sdk build
```

## ALF Task Extension

For AI agent automation, use the ALF Task extension:

```typescript
import { createAlfTaskExtensionV1 } from "@channel.io/app-sdk-server";

export const alfTaskExtension = createAlfTaskExtensionV1({
  getAlfTasks: async (ctx) => ({
    predefinedTasks: [
      {
        version: "v1.0.0",
        name: "Auto Reply",
        trigger: "user_message",
        memorySchema: [
          { name: "userMessage", type: "string" },
          { name: "reply", type: "string" },
        ],
        nodes: [
          {
            id: "analyze",
            type: "function",
            config: { function: "analyzeMessage" },
            next: "reply",
          },
          {
            id: "reply",
            type: "send_message",
            config: { message: "{{memory.reply}}" },
          },
        ],
        startNodeId: "analyze",
      },
    ],
  }),
});
```

## Complete Example

```typescript
// app.module.ts
import { Module } from "@nestjs/common";
import { APP_GUARD } from "@nestjs/core";
import {
  ChannelAppModule,
  SignatureGuard,
  createCalendarExtensionV1,
  createOAuthExtensionV1,
} from "@channel.io/app-sdk-server";

const calendarExtension = createCalendarExtensionV1({
  listEventTypes: async () => ({ eventTypes: [] }),
  getAvailability: async () => ({ slots: [] }),
  createBooking: async () => ({ booking: { id: "1", status: "confirmed" } }),
});

const oauthExtension = createOAuthExtensionV1({
  getAuthConfig: async () => ({
    authType: "oauth",
    authScope: "channel",
    oauthProvider: {
      provider: "example",
      authorizationUrl: "https://example.com/oauth/authorize",
      tokenUrl: "https://example.com/oauth/token",
      scopes: ["read"],
      providerName: "Example",
    },
  }),
  validateCredentials: async (ctx, params) => {
    const accessToken = ctx.authToken ?? params.accessToken;
    return { valid: Boolean(accessToken) };
  },
});

@Module({
  imports: [
    ChannelAppModule.forRoot({
      appId: process.env.APP_ID,
      appSecret: process.env.APP_SECRET,
      signingKey: process.env.SIGNING_KEY,
      extensions: [calendarExtension, oauthExtension],
      autoRegister: true,
      debug: process.env.NODE_ENV === "development",
    }),
  ],
  providers: [{ provide: APP_GUARD, useClass: SignatureGuard }],
})
export class AppModule {}
```
